title: Validating webhook: reject :latest image tags
scenario: Three engineers pushed `nginx:latest` this week, causing unexpected rollbacks when upstream changed. You've been asked to register a ValidatingWebhookConfiguration that blocks pods using `:latest` image tags. The starter config has two bugs: `failurePolicy: Ignore` (lets bad pods through on webhook failure) and only `operations: ["UPDATE"]` (misses new pod creation).
starterYaml:
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingWebhookConfiguration
metadata:
  name: no-latest-images
webhooks:
- name: no-latest.kubeforge.io
  admissionReviewVersions: ["v1"]
  clientConfig:
    service:
      name: webhook-server
      namespace: default
      path: /validate
  rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    apiVersions: ["v1"]
    resources: ["pods"]
    operations: ["UPDATE"]
  failurePolicy: Ignore
  sideEffects: None

hints.text: Two things to fix: `failurePolicy: Ignore` → `Fail`, and add `CREATE` to the operations list.
`failurePolicy: Ignore` means if the webhook server is unavailable, the pod admission is allowed — a security hole. `Fail` blocks pods when the webhook is unreachable.
Change operations to `["CREATE", "UPDATE"]` and failurePolicy to `Fail`.

solution:
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingWebhookConfiguration
metadata:
  name: no-latest-images
webhooks:
- name: no-latest.kubeforge.io
  admissionReviewVersions: ["v1"]
  clientConfig:
    service:
      name: webhook-server
      namespace: default
      path: /validate
  rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    apiVersions: ["v1"]
    resources: ["pods"]
    operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
  failurePolicy: Fail
  sideEffects: None
